questions.q: Rashtrapati Bhavan is the official residence of which office-holder?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan is the official residence of the President of India.

questions.q: In which city is Rashtrapati Bhavan located?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan is located in New Delhi.

questions.q: Which architect is credited with designing Rashtrapati Bhavan?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan was designed by Edwin Lutyens.

questions.q: In which year was Rashtrapati Bhavan completed?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan was completed in 1929.

questions.q: Before independence, Rashtrapati Bhavan was originally built as the residence of whom?
questions.explanation: It was originally built as the residence of the British Viceroy.

questions.q: What does the name 'Rashtrapati Bhavan' broadly mean?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan translates roughly to the House of the President.

questions.q: Rashtrapati Bhavan stands atop which hill in New Delhi?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan stands atop Raisina Hill in New Delhi.

questions.q: The famous gardens at Rashtrapati Bhavan were historically known by which name?
questions.explanation: The gardens were long known as the Mughal Gardens at Rashtrapati Bhavan.

questions.q: Rashtrapati Bhavan was built during which broad period of Indian history?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan was built during the British colonial period and completed in 1929.

questions.q: Rashtrapati Bhavan is located close to which other major government building complex?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan sits adjacent to the Secretariat Buildings on Raisina Hill.

questions.q: Before being renamed Rashtrapati Bhavan, what was the building called during British rule?
questions.explanation: It was known as the Viceroy's House or Government House before being renamed Rashtrapati Bhavan.

questions.q: Who was the first President of India to occupy Rashtrapati Bhavan as an official residence?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad, the first President of India, was the first to occupy Rashtrapati Bhavan.

questions.q: The construction of the Viceroy's House, later Rashtrapati Bhavan, took roughly how long?
questions.explanation: Construction took roughly 17 years, with the building completed in 1929.

questions.q: Rashtrapati Bhavan is renowned for its very large number of rooms, numbering approximately how many?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan has approximately 340 rooms.

questions.q: Which prominent dome is a central architectural feature of Rashtrapati Bhavan, inspired in part by Indian forms?
options: A large central dome over the building
questions.explanation: A large central dome, drawing on Indian and classical influences, crowns Rashtrapati Bhavan.

questions.q: The Durbar Hall inside Rashtrapati Bhavan is used for which kind of occasions?
options: Important ceremonial functions such as swearing-in and award presentations
questions.explanation: The Durbar Hall is used for important ceremonial functions like investitures and award ceremonies.

questions.q: Edwin Lutyens, the architect of Rashtrapati Bhavan, also contributed to the design of which nearby legislative building?
questions.explanation: Lutyens, with Herbert Baker, was also associated with the design of Parliament House.

questions.q: The Mughal Gardens at Rashtrapati Bhavan were officially renamed in 2023 to what?
questions.explanation: In 2023 the Mughal Gardens were renamed Amrit Udyan.

questions.q: Rashtrapati Bhavan lies at one end of the ceremonial boulevard now known as what?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan stands at one end of the ceremonial avenue now called Kartavya Path, formerly Rajpath.

questions.q: The forecourt of Rashtrapati Bhavan features a tall freestanding column known as what?
questions.explanation: The forecourt of Rashtrapati Bhavan contains the Jaipur Column.

questions.q: Rashtrapati Bhavan was constructed mainly using which materials, in keeping with Lutyens's Delhi?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan was built largely of red and cream sandstone, like much of Lutyens's Delhi.

questions.q: Rashtrapati Bhavan also has official retreats associated with the President in which hill stations?
options: Shimla and Hyderabad (the Retreat Building and Rashtrapati Nilayam)
questions.explanation: Presidential retreats include the Retreat Building near Shimla and Rashtrapati Nilayam in Hyderabad.

questions.q: The Viceroy's House was first occupied in 1931 around the time of the formal inauguration of which capital?
questions.explanation: The building was occupied around 1931, coinciding with the formal inauguration of New Delhi as the capital.

questions.q: Approximately how large is the built-up footprint of Rashtrapati Bhavan, making it one of the largest residences of a head of state?
options: Around 200,000 square feet of floor area
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan has an enormous floor area on the order of 200,000 square feet, among the largest residences of any head of state.

questions.q: The central dome of Rashtrapati Bhavan is widely noted to draw inspiration from which ancient Indian structure?
questions.explanation: The dome's form is often compared to the Great Stupa at Sanchi.

questions.q: Rashtrapati Bhavan houses a museum complex opened to give the public access to its heritage; it is broadly known as what?
questions.explanation: A Rashtrapati Bhavan Museum Complex was developed to open parts of the estate's heritage to visitors.

questions.q: Herbert Baker, who worked alongside Lutyens on Raisina Hill, was chiefly responsible for which adjacent buildings?
options: The North and South Block Secretariat Buildings
questions.explanation: Herbert Baker designed the North and South Block Secretariat Buildings flanking Rashtrapati Bhavan.

questions.q: The estate of Rashtrapati Bhavan includes a self-contained township area historically referred to as what?
questions.explanation: The grounds form the President's Estate, a township with residences, offices and amenities for staff.

questions.q: The change of guard and ceremonial guard duties at Rashtrapati Bhavan are performed by which body associated with the President?
questions.explanation: The President's Bodyguard, the senior-most regiment of the Indian Army, performs ceremonial duties at Rashtrapati Bhavan.

questions.q: Rashtrapati Bhavan's main building, the Mughal Gardens area, and the residence are arranged along a strong axis aligned with which monument down the avenue?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan is aligned along the ceremonial axis that runs down the avenue toward India Gate.
